evals.prompt: Decompose spec 002 into factory artifacts. The spec has requirements.md and solution.md already written.
evals.expected_output: Unit specs in units/, scenarios in scenarios/, and a manifest.md with execution order
expectations: Read requirements.md before decomposing
Read solution.md before decomposing
Read AGENTS.md for codebase context
Created at least one unit spec file in units/ directory
Each unit spec has YAML frontmatter with id, title, type, and dependencies fields
Each unit spec has Goal, Requirements, and Constraints sections
Unit specs do NOT contain acceptance criteria or implementation approach
Created at least one scenario file per unit in scenarios/{unit-id}/ directory
Each scenario has YAML frontmatter with unit, feature, and priority fields
Scenarios describe observable behavior through external interfaces only
Scenarios do NOT reference internal implementation details like class names or file paths
Created manifest.md with YAML frontmatter containing title, status, threshold, and max_iterations
Manifest contains Units section with checklist format: - [ ] id: Title — dependency clause
Manifest contains Execution Order section with Group N (parallel|sequential) format
No circular dependencies in the dependency graph
Presented scenarios to user for review before finalizing
Attempted to detect the project's test framework for E2E stub generation
Generated or attempted to generate E2E test stubs alongside scenarios
E2E stubs reference observable behavior through external interfaces, not internal code

evals.prompt: Decompose a feature with no external APIs — a pure refactoring of internal skill files. The requirements and solution docs describe changes to 3 SKILL.md files.
evals.expected_output: Units decomposed by skill file, scenarios test skill behavior, manifest shows independent units
expectations: Units are factory-sized — each can be implemented by a single code agent
Independent units (no shared dependencies) are in the same parallel execution group
Scenarios are grounded in the actual codebase — reference real file paths and patterns
At least one P0 scenario per unit
Manifest threshold defaults to 0.90
Manifest max_iterations defaults to 5
E2E test stubs generated or gracefully skipped if framework undetectable

evals.prompt: The spec directory has requirements.md but no solution.md. Decompose into factory artifacts.
evals.expected_output: Skill detects the missing file, informs the user, and proceeds with available input
expectations: Detected that solution.md is missing
Informed the user that solution.md is absent
Proceeded with decomposition using requirements.md
Created unit specs, scenarios, and manifest despite missing solution.md
Units are derived from requirements since no architecture is available to inform boundaries

evals.prompt: Decompose a complex feature with 8+ components and deep dependency chains between them.
evals.expected_output: Well-structured decomposition with clear dependency graph and multiple execution groups
expectations: Every requirement from requirements.md maps to at least one unit
No unit is too large — each should be implementable in a single session
Dependencies are minimized — shared resources extracted into their own units
Execution groups respect dependency ordering
Coverage matrix shows which requirements map to which units
User was asked to review and approve the decomposition before proceeding to scenarios
